seoTitle: Cockwhy vs Coffee Camp — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cockwhy and Coffee Camp. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cockwhy vs Coffee Camp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cockwhy and Coffee Camp.
heading: Cockwhy vs Coffee Camp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cockwhy, New South Wales and Coffee Camp, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coffee Camp has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Cockwhy (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cockwhy or Coffee Camp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cockwhy has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Coffee Camp scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cockwhy scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Coffee Camp scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cockwhy or Coffee Camp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coffee Camp has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
